verbGlossary.ASSESSED FOR: {source} is evaluated to determine {target} requirements (e.g., project site assessed for environmental permit based on soil, wetland, or habitat conditions).
verbGlossary.CLEARED FOR: {source} receives formal regulatory clearance to be used or occupied (e.g., building cleared for {target} after final inspections pass).
verbGlossary.COMMISSIONED FOR: {source} undergoes third-party performance verification as part of commissioning (e.g., commissioning zone commissioned for third-party testing of HVAC and controls).
verbGlossary.CORRELATES WITH: {source}-based metric has a measured relationship to a regulatory metric (e.g., defect density by location correlates with inspection pass rate across projects).
verbGlossary.COVERED BY: {source} falls within the scope of a regulatory safety plan (e.g., project site covered by site safety plan for fall protection and hazard mitigation).
verbGlossary.DELINEATED FOR: {source} is drawn specifically to satisfy a regulatory review requirement (e.g., fire/smoke compartment delineated for {target} review of rated separations).
verbGlossary.DRAINS TO: {source}'s stormwater runoff is controlled under a specific {target} (e.g., project site drains to stormwater management facilities governed by SWPPP permit).
verbGlossary.ENCROACHES ON: {source} extends into a regulated right-of-way requiring a street or encroachment {target} (e.g., right-of-way work encroaches on public easement requiring street permit).
verbGlossary.FLAGGED FOR: {source}-based deficiency metric triggers a regulatory review action (e.g., firestopping deficiency rate flagged for {target} re-inspection).
verbGlossary.GOVERNS LAYOUT OF: {source} approval dictates the allowable site layout and use (e.g., {target} governs layout of project site including setbacks, height, and density).
verbGlossary.INSPECTED AT: {source} inspector conducts {target} at the specific location (e.g., AHJ inspection performed at each floor level before concealment).
verbGlossary.PERMITTED BY: {source} requires and is authorized by a specific construction {target} (e.g., temporary facility permitted by temporary structures permit before mobilization).
verbGlossary.REGULATED BY: {source}controls activity at {target} (e.g., exterior zone regulated by environmental permit for erosion and sediment control).
verbGlossary.REPORTED TO: {source}-based safety metric triggers a formal notification to {target} (e.g., safety incident density by zone reported to OSHA when thresholds are exceeded).
verbGlossary.REVIEWED BY: {source}-specific plans are examined by {target} (e.g., building plans reviewed by fire marshal for egress, suppression, and alarm compliance).
verbGlossary.SCOPED BY: {source} defines the legal boundaries and allowable work for {target} (e.g., project site scoped by building permit for approved construction limits).
verbGlossary.SEQUENCED BY: Inspection scheduling determines the order in which locations are inspected (e.g., floor levels sequenced by inspection scheduling to align with vertical construction progress).
verbGlossary.STAGED WITHIN: {source}is temporarily positioned within {target}-controlled area (e.g., laydown area staged within street right-of-way permit boundaries).
verbGlossary.SUBMITTED FOR: {source}-specific drawings or plans are filed with {target} for review (e.g., building plans submitted for building department plan review).
verbGlossary.TERMINATED AT: {source}connection endpoints at {target} require a specific permit (e.g., equipment location where utility service is terminated at requires utility connection permit).
